title: Filesystem Integrity
app: Tripwire, OSSEC, WEF → CIM-tagged filesystem events
spl:
index=security tag=endpoint tag=filesystem action=modify
| lookup fim_critical_paths file_path OUTPUT criticality
| where criticality="high"
| stats count by host, file_path
description: FIM events for critical paths (`/etc`, `C:\Windows\System32`) — map vendor FIM to Endpoint `Processes`/`Files` tags or parallel sourcetypes.
value: FIM events for critical paths (`/etc`, `C:\Windows\System32`) — map vendor FIM to Endpoint `Processes`/`Files` tags or parallel sourcetypes.
implementation: Hash diff for high-value files; suppress during patch Tuesday.
cimSpl: | tstats `summariesonly` count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where match(Processes.process_path,"(?i)(etc|System32|SysWOW64)") by Processes.dest, Processes.process_path, _time span=1h
knownFalsePositives: Unusual process, service, and integrity signals from new vendor agents, help-desk tools, and patch cycles; use asset purpose and allowlists in review.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Tripwire, OSSEC, WEF → CIM-tagged filesystem events.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `file_path`, `action=modify`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Hash diff for high-value files; suppress during patch Tuesday.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=security tag=endpoint tag=filesystem action=modify
| lookup fim_critical_paths file_path OUTPUT criticality
| where criticality="high"
| stats count by host, file_path
```

Understanding this SPL

**Filesystem Integrity** — FIM events for critical paths (`/etc`, `C:\Windows\System32`) — map vendor FIM to Endpoint `Processes`/`Files` tags or parallel sourcetypes.

Documented **Data sources**: `file_path`, `action=modify`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Tripwire, OSSEC, WEF → CIM-tagged filesystem events. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: security.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=security, tags. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Filters the current rows with `where criticality="high"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by host, file_path** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats `summariesonly` count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where match(Processes.process_path,"(?i)(etc|System32|SysWOW64)") by Processes.dest, Processes.process_path, _time span=1h
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**Filesystem Integrity** — FIM events for critical paths (`/etc`, `C:\Windows\System32`) — map vendor FIM to Endpoint `Processes`/`Files` tags or parallel sourcetypes.

Documented **Data sources**: `file_path`, `action=modify`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Tripwire, OSSEC, WEF → CIM-tagged filesystem events. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Endpoint.Processes` — enable acceleration for that model.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 — Validate
Accelerate the CIM data model used in the optional tstats path, then run both the primary and `cimSpl` searches over a week that includes a known good baseline. Spot-check a few rows and tune thresholds; confirm no permission or tagging gap is hiding part of the population.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (changes), Timeline.
grandmaExplanation: We use the same kind of check described for "Filesystem Integrity" once our data is shaped in a common, repeatable way across products. We help the team see trouble early, compare different tools fairly, and avoid building a one-off report for every vendor.
dataModelAcceleration: Enable Data Model Acceleration for the CIM `Endpoint.Processes` dataset after normalizing this feed to the model; otherwise the `cimSpl` tstats search may return no rows.
